jokes.text: And I, Dwight Truth I'm in charge of the entire operation.
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to sound authoritative and professional
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces his own name as 'Dwight Truth' instead of 'Dwight Schrute'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Dwight's pompous nature undermined by a simple mistake. Economy and character integration are excellent.

jokes.text: 'Cause I feel like I'm gonna fall off. - Yes. Yes. - I'm not wearing the right shoes for this. - We went over this, okay? Your tiny fingers make the best knots.
jokes.setup: Someone is being held up by another person while doing something precarious
jokes.punchline: The justification is that their 'tiny fingers make the best knots' - absurd logic
jokes.explanation: Mild physical comedy with absurd justification. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Hey, my name's Tabitha. I'm camped out in front of the Sabre store so I can be first in line for the new pyramid. Psst. It's me, Erin.
jokes.setup: A hipster named Tabitha is camping out for the store opening
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually Erin pretending to be a hipster
jokes.explanation: Good setup/reveal structure with character-specific humor. Erin's earnest participation in Dwight's schemes is well-established.

jokes.text: I was born in the little working class town of Basildon, and until the age of 32, I talked like this, which was bloody horrendous, innit?
jokes.setup: Nellie claims she came from humble beginnings
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates her 'working class' accent which is over-the-top
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor playing on Nellie's pretentious nature and fake backstory.

jokes.text: What's lower than dirt? Loam, magma, mantle, outer core, inner core. - Yeah, thank you. - Loam. Bloody loam, I came from.
jokes.setup: Nellie asks what's lower than dirt for dramatic effect
jokes.punchline: Someone provides geological layers, and Nellie latches onto 'loam' dramatically
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation with geological accuracy. 'Bloody loam' is quotable and unexpected.

jokes.text: When I auditioned for the Spice Girls, I didn't even get a callback. Which Spice Girl? The black one. I never stood a chance.
jokes.setup: Nellie shares she auditioned for the Spice Girls and didn't get a callback
jokes.punchline: She wanted to be 'the black one' (Scary Spice) - clearly impossible given she's white
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment with excellent economy. The reveal lands with uncomfortable precision.

jokes.text: Bloggers are gross, bloggers are obese. Bloggers have halitosis. You're gonna love 'em.
jokes.setup: Dwight describing bloggers in preparation for press day
jokes.punchline: After listing horrible traits, cheerfully says 'You're gonna love 'em'
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - brutal honesty delivered with misplaced enthusiasm. Good character integration.

jokes.text: When people see this presentation, they're gonna [Bleep] in their pants. - Okay. - Come on, man. Seriously disgusting.
jokes.setup: Ryan trying to express enthusiasm for his presentation
jokes.punchline: Uses crude expression that disgusts his colleague
jokes.explanation: Relies on crude humor without much craft. Character-appropriate but not clever.

jokes.text: Packer, you will be the sexual predator who has come to prey on the trendy teenage girls who are obsessed with the pyramid.
jokes.setup: Dwight assigning roles for the store opening
jokes.punchline: Assigns Packer to be a 'sexual predator' as if it's a normal marketing role
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy that works because it's so matter-of-fact. Perfectly captures Dwight's tone-deafness.

jokes.text: I don't see what that gets us, but I'm a team player.
jokes.setup: Packer being assigned to play a sexual predator
jokes.punchline: Responds with corporate team-player language to an absurd request
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan response. The corporate speak makes the absurdity funnier.

jokes.text: Schrute's out to get me. But I'm playing the long game. As soon as he messes up, I swoop in like a sexual predator.
jokes.setup: Packer planning his strategy against Dwight
jokes.punchline: Uses the sexual predator metaphor from his assigned role literally
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Packer's literal thinking. Inappropriate metaphor used casually.

jokes.text: I want to create a sense of wonder and enthusiasm as if, at the end of ET, candy poured out of the screen.
jokes.setup: Dwight describing the feeling he wants to create
jokes.punchline: Bizarre mixed metaphor combining ET with candy pouring from screen
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd Dwight metaphor. Original and perfectly character-specific with great quotability.

jokes.text: Speaking of pimples, let's release the bloggers!
jokes.setup: Following his 'goose pimples' comment
jokes.punchline: Transitions to 'pimples' to introduce the gross bloggers
jokes.explanation: Weak pun but in character. Connects to earlier setup about bloggers being gross.

jokes.text: Don't care. Tell me later.
jokes.setup: Someone trying to tell Andy about something exciting that happened
jokes.punchline: Andy dismisses them rudely then immediately asks what happened
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's self-centeredness and contradiction.

jokes.text: I woke up at 4:00 A.M. by accident, in time for the paper to be delivered. Guess what. It's not a kid on a bike. It's a man in a car.
jokes.setup: Jim building up to share an exciting discovery
jokes.punchline: The mundane revelation about newspaper delivery method
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment - building up something mundane as if it's profound. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Hey, I was on the can. What's this about a black guy in the office?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Andy's black eye
jokes.punchline: Meredith mishears 'black eye' as 'black guy'
jokes.explanation: Standard mishearing joke. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Since the interesting thing happened till now, so much time has passed, it's like my life is buffering.
jokes.setup: Frustration about having to wait to hear the story
jokes.punchline: Compares life delay to computer buffering
jokes.explanation: Good modern metaphor that's relatable and well-expressed.

jokes.text: Pam and I were arriving for the day, and there was a gang in the parking lot on bikes, on-- on motorcycles. And they just were hassling Pam... They had, uh... Weapons.
jokes.setup: Jim describing a dangerous encounter
jokes.punchline: The buildup suggests a serious gang situation
jokes.explanation: Setup for larger reveal. Works as escalation but isn't standalone funny.

jokes.text: But these were not the kind of people who use their words. Punches were going, and I ducked a few, landed a couple, and I was fighting them off.
jokes.setup: Continuing the gang fight story
jokes.punchline: Jim describing himself as an action hero
jokes.explanation: Builds suspense for eventual reveal. Jim as action hero is amusing but needs payoff.

jokes.text: I didn't do anything any of you wouldn't have done.
jokes.setup: Jim's heroic fight story
jokes.punchline: Humble conclusion that ironically suggests everyone would fight a gang
jokes.explanation: False modesty that's ironically boastful. Decent character work.

jokes.text: So you're a blogger, right? Yeah. Blogger. God, stay away. Oh, I always get in trouble around bloggers. I'm trying to be a good girl for once.
jokes.setup: Introduction to blogger at the event
jokes.punchline: Cathy treats bloggers like they're dangerous temptation
jokes.explanation: Plays into the established blogger stereotype. Character-appropriate for Cathy.

jokes.text: I haven't had so much fun since seeing... Zoo-ey Desh-channel at the Couch-arilla Music Festival.
jokes.setup: Someone expressing excitement about the event
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'Zooey Deschanel' and 'Coachella' in hipster-trying way
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing try-hard hipster behavior. Mispronunciations are well-chosen.

jokes.text: How can we feel safe knowing that there are gangs here? We should call the police right now! No! No, no, no, no, no, we don't need to call the police.
jokes.setup: Someone wants to call police about the gang
jokes.punchline: Andy desperately trying to prevent police involvement
jokes.explanation: Building tension toward reveal. Andy's panic suggests something's wrong with his story.

jokes.text: Because I have neighborhood pride. 1-8-5-0-5. - 1-8-5-0-5. - Guys, guys. That's so vague. You gotta do the zip plus 4. 1-8-5-0-5... Dash 7-4-2-7!
jokes.setup: Andy claiming neighborhood pride
jokes.punchline: Someone corrects the zip code format with unnecessary precision
jokes.explanation: Absurd focus on zip code precision during crisis. Good escalation of bureaucratic thinking.

jokes.text: Toby is teaching us self-defense. Me? Toby, will you teach us self-defense? Yeah. Um... I can't believe you remembered I do self-defense.
jokes.setup: Andy volunteering Toby to teach self-defense
jokes.punchline: Toby's surprised pleasure that someone remembered his skill
jokes.explanation: Perfect Toby moment - pathetic gratitude for being noticed. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Um, I'll go put on my cup. Great.
jokes.setup: Toby preparing to teach self-defense
jokes.punchline: Overly prepared for what should be basic instruction
jokes.explanation: Good economy and character moment. Toby's over-preparation is funny.

jokes.text: I wasn't sure which one of you was Chuck! Yeah, no, it seems to be going great.
jokes.setup: Someone confused about promotional execution
jokes.punchline: Dwight's sarcastic response to obvious failure
jokes.explanation: Basic sarcasm about failure. Functional but not memorable.

jokes.text: Andy got beat up by a fifth grade girl?
jokes.setup: Revealing the truth about Andy's black eye
jokes.punchline: The 'gang' was actually a fifth grade girl
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to long setup. The reveal completely recontextualizes Andy's heroic story.

jokes.text: This is a perfect photo for my 'Daily Fail' blog. Uh, I-I gotta go. Okay. Dwight, what is a fail? That sounds bad.
jokes.setup: Blogger mentions 'Daily Fail' blog
jokes.punchline: Jim panics and Dwight doesn't understand internet terminology
jokes.explanation: Good character work with Dwight's internet ignorance and Jim's panic.

jokes.text: Oh, no, no, no. It's good. It's really-- on the Internet, it's a really-- That's a really good, good thing.
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to reassure about the 'fail' blog
jokes.punchline: Desperately lying about internet terminology he doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - confident about something he's clueless about.

jokes.text: Hello. Hi, sweetie. It's Jim. I'm calling you from the new Arrowhead, which is why my voice is crystal clear and my hand won't ever get tired because of the ergonomic shape.
jokes.setup: Dwight demonstrating how Jim should have done the phone demo
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top sales pitch pretending to be Jim calling his wife
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - earnest demonstration of terrible sales technique.

jokes.text: You could have pretended to be Chuck. I begged you to pretend to be Chuck, but you chose to be yourself, and you can no longer be Chuck! Surrender the tripack.
jokes.setup: Dwight upset about Jim's failed phone demo
jokes.punchline: Dramatic pronouncement about losing Chuck privileges and 'tripack' surrender
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dwight escalation. 'You can no longer be Chuck' is perfectly absurd and quotable.

jokes.text: Point it towards the store, idiot!
jokes.setup: Someone operating equipment incorrectly
jokes.punchline: Dwight's frustrated direction to point something toward the store
jokes.explanation: Simple frustrated outburst. Visual context probably makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Hey, hey, hey, you guys, you must be lost. Listen. Excuse me, sir. Yeah, the fountain where you can feed the pigeons is out behind the bank. Tell your great grandson to bring his kid by.
jokes.setup: Addressing elderly visitors to the tech store
jokes.punchline: Assumes they're confused and suggests activities for great-great-grandchildren
jokes.explanation: Age-based humor that's somewhat predictable. The great-great-grandchildren reference is excessive.

jokes.text: The elderly suck the life out of the young. Get them out of here! We are closed! Come on.
jokes.setup: Trying to remove elderly customers
jokes.punchline: Bizarre vampire-like metaphor about elderly people
jokes.explanation: Unexpected dark metaphor. Original but not particularly character-specific.

jokes.text: Self-defense is not some fun boxing match, okay? This is about escaping with your life. So... Strike, scream, and run.
jokes.setup: Toby beginning self-defense instruction
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic presentation of basic self-defense
jokes.explanation: Good Toby character work - taking something simple and making it intense.

jokes.text: That may have been my fault. What the hell, Toby?
jokes.setup: Something went wrong during the self-defense demonstration
jokes.punchline: Toby's mild admission followed by angry reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy reaction. Character-appropriate for Toby's incompetence.

jokes.text: So just remember, I-a-a-t-g. 'It's all about the groin.'
jokes.setup: Toby teaching self-defense acronym
jokes.punchline: Awkward acronym that spells out groin advice
jokes.explanation: Good Toby moment - creating unnecessary complexity for simple advice.

jokes.text: What if you're being attacked by a... Smallish man who happens to not have a groin?
jokes.setup: Questioning Toby's groin-focused self-defense
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific hypothetical attacker scenario
jokes.explanation: Absurd Andy logic. The specificity makes it funny.

jokes.text: What if you're being attacked by a 4'11" man who's penisless?
jokes.setup: Following up his previous hypothetical
jokes.punchline: Even more specific and inappropriate scenario
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the absurd scenario. Andy's fixation is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Why are you fixated on this hypothetical transgendered attacker?
jokes.setup: Andy's repeated specific scenarios
jokes.punchline: Toby's confused response to Andy's obsession
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response to Andy's weirdness. Toby trying to be reasonable.

jokes.text: Show us how to defend ourselves against a baby, and then like a fifth-grade girl, and then, you know, if we have time, on up to a scary man.
jokes.setup: Andy suggesting a progression of attackers
jokes.punchline: Starts with baby, includes fifth-grade girl (his actual attacker)
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that reveals Andy's true motivation. Great escalation structure from baby to scary man.

jokes.text: What can the attacker then do to better protect himself? It's interesting that you're drawn to the point of view of the attacker.
jokes.setup: Andy asking how attacker can protect himself
jokes.punchline: Toby noting Andy's suspicious perspective
jokes.explanation: Great character moment revealing Andy's true concern. Toby's observation is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: The latest Chad Flenderman novel... Based from the point of view of his nemesis, Dr. Lucifer Wu.
jokes.setup: Toby defending his interest in attacker perspective
jokes.punchline: References his ridiculous novel with over-the-top villain name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Toby character work. 'Dr. Lucifer Wu' is absurdly specific and quotable.

jokes.text: Can I please leave? I have a rape flute.
jokes.setup: Someone wanting to leave the self-defense class
jokes.punchline: Claims to have a 'rape flute' as protection
jokes.explanation: Incredibly quotable and unexpected. 'Rape flute' is both absurd and economical.

jokes.text: Cathy, I would like to introduce you to fatty gruesome. He is a freelancer for Wired Magazine. Patty Grossman. I'm a woman.
jokes.setup: Dwight introducing someone he assumes is male and gross
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually a woman named Patty Grossman
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment. Dwight's assumptions backfire spectacularly.

jokes.text: Yeah, no [Bleep], Sherlock! Can somebody please tell me something encouraging about this presentation before I go out there in front of a million people and do it?
jokes.setup: Ryan panicking before his presentation
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic response followed by desperate plea for encouragement
jokes.explanation: Standard nervous character moment. Ryan's pretension cracking under pressure.

jokes.text: You just need to realize so much rides on this. - You have no idea.
jokes.setup: Ryan asking for encouragement
jokes.punchline: Dwight makes him more nervous by emphasizing the pressure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - completely missing what Ryan needs.

jokes.text: I'm trying to make him feel important. God. I wish Kelly were here. She always knew what to say.
jokes.setup: Dwight's failed attempt at encouragement
jokes.punchline: Realizes he needs Kelly's help with psychology
jokes.explanation: Shows Dwight's self-awareness and need for Kelly's emotional intelligence.

jokes.text: Oh, Ryan, you're so smart. You're smarter than Mark Zuckerberg and those Google guys all combined. - Hee hee hee. - You're so ignorant, you barely know what you're talking about.
jokes.setup: Someone trying to boost Ryan's confidence Kelly-style
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between fake encouragement and harsh reality.

jokes.text: What's a book? Oh, my God. You're so embarrassing.
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting Ryan read books
jokes.punchline: Response reveals complete ignorance about books
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective portrayal of anti-intellectual attitude.

jokes.text: You can do it, Ryan. And you know that I'm capable of this. - You're the only one who can do it, s-sweetie.
jokes.setup: Attempting maternal encouragement for Ryan
jokes.punchline: Awkward role-playing as his mother
jokes.explanation: Awkward attempt at nurturing that falls flat.

jokes.text: Oh, 'fix' means you hate it! I knew it! - I need something to drink!
jokes.setup: Someone offering gentle criticism
jokes.punchline: Ryan spirals into panic over the word 'fix'
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Ryan's fragile ego and artistic pretension.

jokes.text: No, not a water. A sports drink. I hate everything in that fridge. Not red-- Get me something yellow or green from a nearby store. Not red!
jokes.setup: Ryan needing a drink to calm down
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific and demanding requirements for drink colors
jokes.explanation: Excellent character escalation. Ryan's pretentious specificity is perfect.

jokes.text: Go to a nearby store and get him a yellow or green sports drink! Now, if your attacker is willing to defile a corpse, you better stop playing dead right away and just make it known that you're alive.
jokes.setup: Jim following Ryan's drink demands, cut to Toby's self-defense
jokes.punchline: Toby's extremely dark self-defense scenario
jokes.explanation: Incredibly dark and unexpected from Toby. The juxtaposition with drink demands makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I believe my daughter had an altercation with somebody here, some fancy gentleman with a squeaky voice? I think you guys might have the wrong office. That's him... The guy I hit.
jokes.setup: Parent looking for someone who fought their daughter
jokes.punchline: Andy is revealed as the victim, girl identifies him as who she hit
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the setup. Andy's humiliation is complete.

jokes.text: What about the lady you hit with a pine cone? There. That chubby one. I just had a baby. - Yesterday?
jokes.setup: Girl identifying another victim
jokes.punchline: Points to Phyllis, who reveals she just had a baby (explaining weight)
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the girl's reign of terror. Phyllis's reveal is well-timed.

jokes.text: Sorry I kicked your ass in front of your 'thin' girlfriend. How 'bout we wait till next year after you have your kid?
jokes.setup: Girl apologizing to Andy
jokes.punchline: Backhanded apology that continues to mock him and Phyllis
jokes.explanation: Excellent non-apology that makes things worse. The 'thin' in quotes is perfect.

jokes.text: There's no shame in getting beaten up by a girl. My ex-wife used to demolish me. No, there is shame in it, okay? We have to draw the line somewhere.
jokes.setup: Toby trying to comfort Andy
jokes.punchline: Toby's over-sharing about domestic abuse, Andy's horrified response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Toby over-sharing followed by Andy's reasonable but harsh response.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I think I see the imprint of a ring pop.
jokes.setup: Examining Andy's injuries
jokes.punchline: The weapon was a children's candy ring
jokes.explanation: Perfect detail that makes Andy's defeat even more humiliating. Ring pop as weapon is brilliant.

jokes.text: Hey, Uncle Lucas, it's your nephew, Ryan. Honestly, I could use a prescription for ritalin right now. Well, I know you did one for Aunt Carol. Oh, so it's different because it's your wife?
jokes.setup: Ryan calling his uncle for prescription drugs
jokes.punchline: Arguing about family favoritism regarding illegal prescriptions
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Ryan's desperation and entitlement.

jokes.text: Don't talk to me right now. I'm sorry, I know you're my boss, but seriously, you need to get the hell out of my face.
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to manage panicking Ryan
jokes.punchline: Ryan completely melting down and insulting his boss
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ryan's breakdown. Character-appropriate meltdown.

jokes.text: Your little man is unraveling. Now, go and fix it.
jokes.setup: Watching Ryan's meltdown
jokes.punchline: Nellie's matter-of-fact assessment and command
jokes.explanation: Good Nellie moment - authoritative and dismissive. 'Little man' is condescending.

jokes.text: Someone rejected you? With that body and those bazongas? Forget him! Yeah! Forget him! And you should forget your husband. Well...
jokes.setup: Someone encouraging Erin about Andy
jokes.punchline: Accidentally tells her to forget her dead husband too
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that goes too far. The awkward pause after 'well...' sells it.

jokes.text: 'I'm sorry. I lied. I'm not in the bathroom. I can't do it. I need to see my mom. I'm going home.'
jokes.setup: Ryan supposedly in bathroom before presentation
jokes.punchline: Text reveals he fled to his mother
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan moment - complete failure and running to mommy. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: You are gonna bloody ruin it. You're gonna bloody ruin it because you're a no-good half-ass cockeyed...
jokes.setup: Nellie panicking about Jim doing the presentation
jokes.punchline: Her working-class accent returns under stress with creative insults
jokes.explanation: Good callback to her earlier accent reveal. Stress brings out her true voice.

jokes.text: Look at me. Look at me! Look... At... This... Face. This is not the face of a performer. This is the face of a scary apparition you see before you die.
jokes.setup: Jim trying to get out of doing the presentation
jokes.punchline: Dramatic description of his own face as death omen
jokes.explanation: Great Jim moment - dramatic self-deprecation. 'Scary apparition you see before you die' is very quotable.

jokes.text: If you don't do this, I don't stand a chance. Please, Jim.
jokes.setup: Dwight's desperation about the presentation
jokes.punchline: Rare moment of Dwight showing vulnerability and begging Jim
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dwight's rare vulnerability. Effective emotional beat.

jokes.text: There's nothing like some last-minute changes to really energize a presentation. Huh?
jokes.setup: Jim dealing with sudden presentation responsibility
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about last-minute changes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim sarcasm. Captures his ability to find humor in bad situations.

jokes.text: I wanna watch you get dressed. Did you find the eyeliner? I'm not wearing eyeliner. You are wearing eyeliner, Jim.
jokes.setup: Jim changing for the presentation
jokes.punchline: Dwight insisting Jim wear eyeliner for tech presentation
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight moment - his bizarre vision of tech presentation style.

jokes.text: Time. Space. Gender. There are no rules anymore. All boundaries are breaking down in the wake of the infinite future.
jokes.setup: Jim starting the presentation
jokes.punchline: Pretentious tech-bro speak about breaking boundaries
jokes.explanation: Good parody of tech presentation speak. Jim doing his best with bad material.

jokes.text: The pyramids-- The strongest shape ever constructed, a shape that fits all other shapes inside of it. No, that-- It's true.
jokes.setup: Jim describing the pyramid product
jokes.punchline: Obviously false claim that pyramids fit all shapes inside them
jokes.explanation: Absurd pseudo-scientific claim delivered with false confidence.

jokes.text: I've been through a lot of issues in my life. I've seen drug addiction, unemployment. I've been in a relationship that tore my heart apart without ever being able to accept that love drove the pain.
jokes.setup: Jim's presentation taking personal turn
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic personal confession during product demo
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment. Jim making up dramatic backstory on the spot.

jokes.text: When I was ten years old, my parents took me to Disney World. I cried the whole time. I was not able to comprehend the beauty that was before me. I just wanted... To go home.
jokes.setup: Jim continuing his fake emotional story
jokes.punchline: Pretentious interpretation of crying at Disney World
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd backstory. The Disney World crying story is perfectly pretentious and quotable.

jokes.text: You can play anything from Chuck to Cars 2.
jokes.setup: Jim describing the pyramid's capabilities
jokes.punchline: Range from obscure TV show to bad movie sequel
jokes.explanation: Great callback to earlier Chuck reference. The range from Chuck to Cars 2 is perfectly random.

jokes.text: Oh!
jokes.setup: Something goes wrong with the presentation
jokes.punchline: Jim's surprised reaction to technical failure
jokes.explanation: Basic reaction beat. Physical comedy that relies on visual context.

jokes.text: All right. Thank you so much. Wow. Wow! And good night. Yeah. Thank you. Thank you very much.
jokes.setup: Jim ending his disastrous presentation
jokes.punchline: Over-enthusiastic conclusion to obvious failure
jokes.explanation: Good ending to the cringe sequence. Jim's false enthusiasm is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: little things can be dangerous. Whether it's a Gremlin or Chucky The Doll-- The key is to throw it in something. Like a fireplace or a tub of electricity.
jokes.setup: Andy giving self-defense advice about small attackers
jokes.punchline: 'Tub of electricity' as a disposal method
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd phrase. 'Tub of electricity' is completely nonsensical and incredibly quotable.

jokes.text: Good point? What is a tub of electricity? - I don't-- I don't--
jokes.setup: Someone praising Andy's electricity tub advice
jokes.punchline: Toby questioning the impossible concept
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response to Andy's absurdity. Toby trying to inject logic.

jokes.text: It's a 12-year-old female bully. - I was a 12-year-old bully. - Great! I think Kelly should attack Toby.
jokes.setup: Identifying the specific threat they're defending against
jokes.punchline: Kelly volunteers her bullying experience for demonstration
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Kelly. Sets up the physical comedy to follow.

jokes.text: You think you're so pretty! Well, you're not gonna be so pretty come prom time! Okay, this is what's called pre-violent posturing.
jokes.setup: Kelly demonstrating bullying behavior
jokes.punchline: Toby trying to narrate while being threatened
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Kelly's authentic bullying and Toby's academic narration.

jokes.text: Take that! Not so pre-violent anymore! Okay, I'm at what's called 'the decision point.'
jokes.setup: Kelly attacking Toby during demonstration
jokes.punchline: Toby continuing to narrate while being attacked
jokes.explanation: Excellent physical comedy with Toby maintaining his analytical narration during assault.

jokes.text: God! My good eye! Oh, boy!
jokes.setup: Andy getting hit during the chaos
jokes.punchline: His remaining good eye gets injured
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalating Andy's injury situation. 'My good eye' is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: You know why I got hit by girls? Because I stood up for others... Pam and Toby. I stepped in, and I didn't care that I was standing up to girls.
jokes.setup: Andy defending his actions
jokes.punchline: Reframing his defeats as heroic stands
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's ability to reframe failure as heroism.

jokes.text: You may wanna ask yourselves, 'where were you when the girls came?'
jokes.setup: Andy continuing his heroic reframing
jokes.punchline: Dramatic challenge that sounds like a war memorial
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation. 'Where were you when the girls came?' is perfectly absurd and quotable.

jokes.text: Tough day. Yes. But I feel good. I put the office in their place, took a bunch of pain killers, drank half a bottle of wine, took my pants off. I just feel good!
jokes.setup: Andy reflecting on his day
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly concerning coping mechanisms
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Andy's concerning coping methods. The pants detail is perfect.

jokes.text: We could have integrated more Chuck into the presentation. Dwight. You're the Vice President.
jokes.setup: Jim admitting presentation flaws
jokes.punchline: Despite everything, Dwight gets promoted
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier failure. Dwight's success despite disaster is appropriate.

jokes.text: Ha ha ha ha! Yeah! Okay! - Come on! - Aah! Right on! Bam, bam, bam, bam, bam! Boom!
jokes.setup: Dwight celebrating his promotion
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top celebration with sound effects
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight victory celebration. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.
